  "clarification_question": "Which scope should we take to \"make this stronger\"? A) Sentence-level punch-up only (clarity, concision, flow; claims unchanged). B) Strengthen framing and structure (reorder, sharpen thesis and contributions, tighten claim–evidence linkage; may rephrase claim strength). C) Additionally revisit novelty positioning and citations to avoid/justify \"first\" claims.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening hinges on sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level.",
    "R1.2": "A stronger read requires a clean, checkable argument chain from assumptions to theorem to corollaries.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about calibration and Brier score should visibly align with the reported theory and empirical summaries.",
    "R1.5": "Reordering and tightening section-level flow can foreground the main contributions and improve narrative impact.",
    "R1.6": "Crisper sentences, cadence, and concision directly increase perceived strength without altering content.",
    "RX.1": "Clarifying assumptions and limits improves credibility while avoiding false certainty.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overreach (e.g., \"first formal analysis\") is critical when intensifying claims.",
    "RX.3": "Work will stay confined to strengthening the provided text per the selected option, not changing technical results.",
    "RX.4": "Careful framing reduces risk of misuse stemming from overstated reliability or generality.",
    "RX.5": "Edits will preserve traceability so that each strengthened claim remains grounded in the given artifact."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope should we take to "make this stronger"? A) Sentence-level punch-up only (clarity, concision, flow; claims unchanged). B) Strengthen framing and structure (reorder, sharpen thesis and contributions, tighten claim–evidence linkage; may rephrase claim strength). C) Additionally revisit novelty positioning and citations to avoid/justify "first" claims.

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening hinges on sharpening the core thesis and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: A stronger read requires a clean, checkable argument chain from assumptions to theorem to corollaries.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about calibration and Brier score should visibly align with the reported theory and empirical summaries.
rationale.R1.5: Reordering and tightening section-level flow can foreground the main contributions and improve narrative impact.
rationale.R1.6: Crisper sentences, cadence, and concision directly increase perceived strength without altering content.
rationale.RX.1: Clarifying assumptions and limits improves credibility while avoiding false certainty.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overreach (e.g., "first formal analysis") is critical when intensifying claims.
rationale.RX.3: Work will stay confined to strengthening the provided text per the selected option, not changing technical results.
rationale.RX.4: Careful framing reduces risk of misuse stemming from overstated reliability or generality.
rationale.RX.5: Edits will preserve traceability so that each strengthened claim remains grounded in the given artifact.
